abstract: Ayurveda, an ancient holistic medicinal system, has sustained its relevance over millennia and continues to be practised extensively in India. However, challenges persist in clinical expertise among Ayurvedic practitioners, often due to limited clinical exposure during their academic journey. The Ayurveda Clinical E-Learning Portal (AyurCeL) aims to bridge the gap in clinical expertise among Ayurvedic practitioners by providing a digital platform for sharing well-documented clinical case studies. This paper outlines the objectives, architecture, and comprehensive modules of AyurCeL, emphasizing its role in facilitating continuous clinical learning and improving the quality of Ayurvedic practice. AyurCeL offers a multi-faceted approach to learning, featuring a clinical case repository, Ayurveda case report preprint archive and post-publication review tool. By merging traditional insights with contemporary technology, AyurCeL not only preserves Ayurveda's profound legacy but also enhances its relevance and reach in today's medical landscape.

AyurCeL's significance extends beyond merely being a repository of clinical cases.

With India being home to an extensive network of Ayurvedic educational institutions and practitioners, it presents a vast reservoir of clinical knowledge and expertise.
The Ayurveda Clinical E-Learning Portal (AyurCel) was developed to address this gap.

The objectives of AyurCeL are as follows.1.To develop a web platform for sharing the clinical experiences of clinicians to facilitate continued clinical learning in the field of Ayurveda.2.To assist clinicians in developing clinical case narratives from day-to-day clinical practice.3.To develop an authoring tool for adding clinical cases to the web modules.4.To develop an interactive user interface for e-learning using the content of the clinical cases uploaded in the database.5.To facilitate the publication of clinical experiences of Ayurveda physicians as preprints prior to submission in regular journals.
A major objective of AyurCeL is to furnish a digital platform for sharing meticulously documented clinical case studies under the supervision of research mentors.

text:
Introduction
Ayurveda, a holistic medicinal system rooted in India's ancient Vedic tradition, has been practised for more than 5000 years. It places emphasis on maintaining harmony between the body, mind, and environment. With India being home to an extensive network of Ayurvedic educational institutions and practitioners, it presents a vast reservoir of clinical knowledge and expertise.

Ayurveda represents a remarkable continuity of clinical practice spanning across centuries, which has survived into modern times [1]. Yet, conducting rigorous clinical trials to encompass its diverse practices remains a significant challenge [2]. However, leveraging real-world evidence gathered from point-of-care settings offers a promising alternative for initial documentation of Ayurvedic practices [3]. Data from real-world clinical practice will serve as a valuable resource for clinical education and can guide the design of more comprehensive studies and trials; thereby supporting Evidence-based education and Evidence-based clinical practice.

Current Ayurvedic practitioners often encounter limited clinical exposure throughout their academic journey [4]. The Ayurveda Clinical E-Learning Portal (AyurCel) was developed to address this gap.

The objectives of AyurCeL are as follows.1.To develop a web platform for sharing the clinical experiences of clinicians to facilitate continued clinical learning in the field of Ayurveda.2.To assist clinicians in developing clinical case narratives from day-to-day clinical practice.3.To develop an authoring tool for adding clinical cases to the web modules.4.To develop an interactive user interface for e-learning using the content of the clinical cases uploaded in the database.5.To facilitate the publication of clinical experiences of Ayurveda physicians as preprints prior to submission in regular journals.

A major objective of AyurCeL is to furnish a digital platform for sharing meticulously documented clinical case studies under the supervision of research mentors. This portal emphasizes the significance of experiential learning, enabling practitioners to gain insights from real-world clinical scenarios. This provides a crucial understanding of the practical applications and potential risks or benefits of medical products, particularly in complex Ayurvedic treatments.

AyurCeL's significance extends beyond merely being a repository of clinical cases. It embodies a vision to improve the quality of Ayurvedic practice by fostering a culture of continuous clinical learning. The platform architecture incorporates state-of-the-art E-learning best practices, ensuring that it remains scalable and adaptable to the evolving needs of its user base.

AyurCeL offers tools to streamline the publication of top cases from the platform as case reports in journals. Through resources like the Ayurveda case report preprint archive and the post-publication review tool, AyurCeL advocates for transparent and rigorous scientific reporting within the Ayurvedic domain. The preprint server expedites the publication and dissemination of clinical practice findings before they appear in journals while the post-publication review tool assists in identifying gaps in the current publication of case reports.

AyurCeL assumes significance in the wake of the efforts of the Ministry of Ayush to embrace digitalisation for education and research in the sector [5]. The use of digital technology to enhance the efficiency of clinical services witnessed a surge during the COVID-19 pandemic [6].

Learning from clinical experience is crucial for Ayurvedic practitioners, especially given the limited clinical exposure during formal training. AyurCeL aims to convert clinical experiences into structured data, thereby facilitating interactive learning. The platform only includes well-documented cases with established modern and Ayurvedic diagnoses which may also play a role in education in Ayurveda-focused integrative medicine practice. It is designed to serve as a repository and learning environment for Ayurvedic practitioners, provide visibility for clinicians, and foster communication within the Ayurveda community.

The architecture is designed to be modular, scalable, and secure, adhering to best practices on e-learning platforms.

AyurCeL serves as a platform for clinicians to showcase their expertise by creating profiles that highlight successful cases they have handled. In the future, institutions could leverage this platform to build exclusive profiles, emphasizing their accomplishments in treating specific conditions. Clinical data repositories like AyurCeL can help in the development of pattern-discovery-based outcome prediction tools and risk modelling for specific diseases [7].

There are many challenges faced by clinicians in rigorous reporting of outcomes of their clinical practice. It is a challenge for clinicians to meticulously document the details of each case they are treating and then develop case narratives. It is even more challenging to prepare case reports that meet reporting guidelines like CARE statement for submission to research journals. AyurCeL offers the services of a dedicated team that will assist clinicians in developing case narratives that comply with the CARE checklist. These case narratives are deposited in the AyurCeL portal and also published as preprints. The clinicians can convert these preprints to full-fledged case reports that can be published in mainstream journals. The AyurCeL team works with the clinicians by providing checklists, and structured formats for the preliminary compilation of data and walks them through the submission of case details. This process ensures that all cases submitted to AyurCeL meet the CARE guidelines and follow a standardised format. Once the case details are submitted, the AyurCeL team develops each case into an interactive learning module and a preprint. At present, there is no other portal that offers these services to clinicians.

This feature is beneficial even for the public, guiding them to identify doctors who have successfully treated conditions of interest. This helps individuals to identify experienced professionals listed on the platform.

While the AyurCeL platform aligns with Sustainable Development Goal 3 (SDG3) of ensuring healthy lives and promoting well-being for all at all ages, it also aligns with Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG4), promoting inclusive and equitable access to quality education. AyurCeL contributes to realizing several SDG4 targets as well. Furthermore, it addresses the imperative to augment the supply of qualified teachers, especially in developing countries, through international cooperation for teacher training. AyurCeL plays a pivotal role in enhancing educational quality and fostering lifelong learning opportunities.

Architecture of AyurCeL platform
AyurCeL platform consists of three independent components - the AyurCeL Clinical Case Repository, Ayurveda Case Report Preprint Archive and Post Publication Review Tool (See Fig. 1).Fig. 1Fig. 1 Architecture of AyurCel PlatformFig. 1

The AyurCeL clinical case repository
The main component of AyurCeL Platform is the Clinical Case Repository which showcases clinical cases submitted by a panel of contributors, which are subjected to a rigorous process of peer review before inclusion.

Protocol for clinical case submission
To maintain a high quality, a rigorous methodology was designed for inviting, scrutinising, and integrating clinical case experiences into the AyurCeL clinical case repository. The workflow includes multiple steps, from constituting panels for contribution and review to the technical aspects of data classification and user interface development. The criteria for case selection ensure scientific rigour and ethical compliance.

AyurCeL offers a helping hand to busy clinicians who do not have the time and resources to write and publish detailed case reports. Clinicians can contact the AyurCeL team expressing interest in submitting a case. Using a checklist, the team confirms that the clinician has a case to share that meets the inclusion criteria. The physician can provide a summary of the potential case by way of voice clips or text messages with supporting documents. The AyurCeL team will organise this information into a structured summary making sure that the content complies with the CARE guidelines for publishing case reports. The structured summary of the case is then reviewed by an expert panel of reviewers. Accepted cases are included in the AyurCeL clinical case repository by creating interactive content and the case narrative is published as a preprint in the Ayurveda Case Report Preprint Archive.

AyurCeL's efficacy is contingent on its hosts' quality and diversity of clinical cases. This outlines the detailed stepwise workflow for submitting and integrating clinical cases into the AyurCeL platform.

Workflow for clinical case integration
The workflow for incorporating clinical cases into AyurCeL comprises forming an expert panel, followed by Case Screening, Case Selection, Data Classification, and Content Creation (See Fig. 2).Phase 1: Panel ConstitutionFig. 2Fig. 2 Workflow for clinical case integration of AyurCeL Case RepositoryFig. 2

Panel of Contributors: A dynamic panel comprising experienced Ayurvedic clinicians was constituted. These contributors share their clinical experiences in a predefined format.

Expert Panel for Peer Review: An expert panel is constituted to review the submitted cases and the overall quality of the web platform.Phase 2: Case Screening

Screening of Cases: Submissions made by the panel of contributors are screened to check compliance with CARE guidelines for publication of case reports.Phase 3: Case Selection

Cases submitted by clinicians are included in the AyurCeL database based on stringent inclusion and exclusion criteria as detailed below.

Inclusion Criteria.●Significant clinical outcomes post-Ayurvedic intervention.●Established biomedical diagnosis with supporting documentation.●Well-documented treatment outcomes with objective findings.●Absence of confounding factors affecting the clinical outcome.●Clinical outcomes significantly divergent from the natural course of the disease or predicted outcomes from other treatments.

Exclusion Criteria.●Inadequate documentation, particularly unclear diagnosis or lack of supportive documentation.●Absence of patient consent for case publication.Phase 4: Data Classification

Data Classification: Data from the clinical case narratives are classified under specific data heads and subheads for uniformity.

Data Entry: The classified data will be entered into the AyurCeL authoring tool.Phase 5: Content Creation

Content Creation: Content is organised in an interactive interface with supporting multimedia, illustrations and tools for immersive learning.

Authoring Tool: A specialised authoring tool captures the classified clinical case data.

User Interface: An interactive user interface facilitates E-learning based on the selected clinical cases.

System architecture and technical specifications
The platform is intended for Ayurveda doctors, practitioners, and students, offering a range of learning resources based on case studies.

The architecture of AyurCeL is designed to be robust, scalable, and user-friendly, ensuring seamless interaction for both practitioners and students of Ayurveda.

The platform is built on a multi-tier architecture comprising the front-end, back-end, and database layers, each serving a specific function in the system.

The front end is developed using HTML5, CSS, and Bootstrap, providing a responsive and intuitive user interface. jQuery and Ajax are used for client-side scripting, enabling dynamic content loading and enhancing user experience.

The core framework used for the back-end is ProcessWire 3.0, an open-source CMS and framework. It provides the necessary functionalities for content management, user authentication, and data manipulation.

AyurCeL clinical case repository features and functionalities
AyurCeL Clinical Case Repository consists of three modules designed to serve specific educational and informational needs of the users. This makes it a comprehensive and user-friendly platform for continuous clinical learning in Ayurveda. These include 1) Case Summaries: Provide quick browsing options through audio clips, videos, graphical abstracts, and narratives. 2) Case Details: Allows detailed case exploration based on biomedical or Ayurvedic diagnosis. And 3) Case Queries: Offers advanced search options for specific queries.

There is also a back-end Administrative Module with case authoring tools.

Case Summaries odule
Listen - Audio Narratives of Clinical Cases: This sub-module provides audio narratives of approximately 2–3 min for each clinical case. The audio format facilitates easy consumption of case information, particularly beneficial for clinicians with time constraints.

Watch - Video Narratives of Clinical Cases: Short video narratives offer visual insights into the clinical cases, enhancing the learning experience by providing visual context to the audio narratives.

View - Graphical Abstracts of Clinical Cases: This sub-module presents graphical abstracts that summarize the diagnosis and treatment outcomes, enabling quick comprehension of the case details.

Read - Summary Narratives of Clinical Cases: Textual abstracts offer a concise written summary of each clinical case for those who prefer reading.

Case etails odule
Abstracts: Provides a succinct clinical case summary, including key findings and outcomes.

Quick Browsing: Allows users to skim through the case details quickly.

Timelines: A chronological representation of the case, from diagnosis to treatment and outcomes.

Tabulated Summaries: Presents key information in a tabular format for easy reference.

Case Narrative: A detailed case narrative, including diagnostic methods, treatment plans, and outcomes.

Immersive Learning Sections: Provides in-depth information on biomedical and Ayurvedic diagnoses, treatment details, and outcome measures.

This module offers detailed medical cases treated with Ayurveda, presented through various mediums such as graphical abstracts, and audio and video narratives. Each case includes a detailed timeline and tabulated summary. The summary section provides a tabular detail of the case. Narrative sections have a detailed narration of the case. Immersive Learning provides the diagnosis, treatment, and outcome of treatment (See Fig. 3).Fig. 3Fig. 3 Interactive modules of AyurceL Clinical Case RepositoryFig. 3

Visual representation of treatment timelines is crucial for understanding the chronological sequence of medical interventions, medications, and outcomes. Timelines offer a quick and intuitive way to grasp complex treatment details. One of the key features of this platform is the ability to represent treatment timelines visually. Admin users can add, view, edit, and delete treatment details for each case, generating timeline images based on these details.

Pre-designed templates for the timeline images are included. A template includes placeholders for important events, start and end dates, medications, procedures, and other relevant information. The template is dynamically populated with treatment data retrieved from the AyurCeL database. This ensures that each timeline image is tailored to the specific treatment details of each case.

Case Queries odule
General Queries: Allows users to ask broad questions about treatments and outcomes for specific conditions.

Disease Search: Enables users to query based on specific diseases, providing information on previous treatments and outcomes.

Formulation/Therapy/Herb Search: Allows users to search based on specific herbs, formulations, or therapies to understand their application across various diseases.

Adverse Events: Enables users to query any reported adverse events associated with specific Ayurvedic interventions.

Concomitant Medicines: Provides information on any additional medicines administered during the treatment.

Administrative Modules
User Management: AyurCeL employs Processwire's default authentication registration module, with custom certificate verification requirements and role-based access control.

Case Management: Admin users can add, view, edit, and delete cases and manage contributors and reviewers.

Security Measures: Role-based access control for different user roles such as Super Admin, Guest, Moderator, etc. Email and certificate verification for user authentication.

Ayurveda Case Reports Preprint Server (AyurCaseRxiv)
Preprint archives allow rapid dissemination of research findings before peer review, facilitating community feedback [8]. The Ayurveda Care Reports Preprint Server facilitates quick publication of case report drafts, assigning a unique DOI (Digital Object Identifier) to each preprint. The Preprint archive for Ayurveda Case Reports is hosted on the Open Preprint Systems (OPS) platform of the Public Knowledge Project [9]. The preprint archive allows authors to submit their case report drafts through a step-by-step wizard. An editorial team approves the preprints before they are shared for public access. The preprints are not subjected to peer review before publication.

Post-publication review tool
This component of AyurCeL is a tool for post-publication review of published case reports in Ayurveda. It evaluates published case reports for compliance with CARE guidelines, providing a quality score for each case report. The Care Statement is a checklist of 13 topics and 30 items. Based on the CARE Statement, a 56-item checklist was developed for reviewers to assess the quality of case reports published in peer-reviewed indexed research journals. Reviewers are Ayurveda researchers who express interest in participating in post-publication reviews of published case reports. The results of the review can be accessed and viewed by medical professionals. Readers can also contribute to the review process by submitting their comments to the moderator. This utility helps in identifying the gaps in the publication of high-quality case reports and serves as a tool for training clinicians to critically appraise published case reports and to write and publish case reports with more scientific rigour.

Integration of the three components of AyurCeL platform
The three components of the AyurCeL Platform are seamlessly integrated to provide an enriching educational experience in clinical learning. The users can learn from the collection of clinical cases through an interactive interface or by reading the case narratives. In addition, they can also identify gaps in the quality of published case reports and improve case report writing. (See Fig. 4).Fig. 4Fig. 4 Workflow of AyurCeL PlatformFig. 4

Current functionality of AyurCeL platform
The AyurCeL project was funded by Rashtriya Ayurved Vidyapeeth, Ministry of Ayush, Government of India and the platform was built and populated with content over a period of 32 months (April 15, 2021, to December 15, 2023). The AyurCeL clinical case repository has a collection of 100 clinical cases contributed by a panel of 46 Ayurveda physicians. The clinical cases compiled in the AyurCeL clinical case repository are being converted into preprints for publication in the Ayurveda Case Reports Preprint Server. The Preprint Server is available and accepts preprints for rapid publication (http://ayurcaserxiv.org/index.php/ayucaserxive). The Post Publication Review Tool has a collection of more than 200 published case reports, which have been assessed and scored for quality based on compliance with the CARE checklist (See Fig. 5). The entire platform is online (https://ayurcel.org/) with restricted access for the public and full access for Ayurveda physicians and students after registration.Fig. 5Fig 5 Potential outputs of AyurCeL PlatformFig. 5

As of now, 191 physicians, 72 students, and 28 members of the public have registered and accessed the platform.

Discussion
The AyurCeL portal is designed to address a critical gap in the clinical training of Ayurvedic practitioners. The platform's architecture and functionalities are tailored to facilitate sharing clinical experiences, thereby serving as a repository of well-documented case studies. AyurCeL will serve as a valuable tool for continued medical education programs in Ayurveda for nurturing diverse clinical skills amongst the practitioners of Ayurveda.

Rastogi opines that clinical speciality-based studies and practices are absent in present Ayurvedic education and practice [10]. Patwardhan et al. have pointed out the lack of exposure to basic clinical skills among Ayurveda graduates as a serious flaw in professional Ayurvedic education [11]. [12]. emphasise that clinical practice guidelines based on consensus of practitioners must be included in the curriculum. The AyurCeL platform is built from the contributions of experienced Ayurveda clinicians across India. AyurCeL is more than a mere clinical case repository. The AyurCeL team offers help and support to clinicians to identify clinical cases from their practice and develop clinical case narratives that comply with CARE guidelines. Upscaling of AyurCeL will generate data that can help in better understanding the strengths of Ayurveda to fill gaps in the health care system.

AyurCeL enables clinicians to share their clinical experiences with the clinical case repository with minimal effort. The platform has a physician-friendly interface for submission of clinical data facilitated by a team of researchers who assist the physicians throughout the process of submission, peer review and acceptance/rejection of a clinical case. Physicians need to only provide the core clinical data, whereas the interactive content and case narrative are developed by the AyurCeL team. The interactive nature of the AyurCeL platform sets it apart from traditional methods of clinical knowledge dissemination. The platform's query functions, ranging from general to specific, provide a dynamic learning environment. The Ayush Clinical Case Repository is an initiative of the Ministry of Ayush launched in 2021 (https://accr.ayush.gov.in). This portal has a collection of 28 case studies but is limited to providing a brief summary of each case. AyurCeL has now been integrated with ACCR as it offers a more comprehensive and multimedia-supported environment for clinical case documentation and clinical training. ACCR accepts clinical cases from all Ayush systems, whereas AyurCeL is at present dedicated to Ayurveda. Visual Representation in Clinical Learning enhances the cognitive assimilation of complex clinical data. Visual timelines offer a more intuitive understanding of the sequence and interplay of various treatment modalities, medications, and outcomes. This is particularly beneficial for Ayurvedic practitioners, who often employ a multi-modal approach to treatment involving a combination of internal medicines, external therapies, diet and lifestyle modifications.

The stringent criteria for case inclusion ensure that only well-documented and authentic cases are part of the AyurCeL database. The platform also offers tutorial modules that guide clinicians on proper documentation and publication, elevating the overall quality of clinical case reports in the field. This focus on quality aligns with the imperative of enhanced clinical documentation in Ayurveda [13]. The timeline feature also has the potential to foster interdisciplinary collaboration. Providing a standardised, visual representation of Ayurvedic treatments makes it easier for practitioners from other medical disciplines to understand the Ayurvedic approach to treatment. This could be a stepping stone toward more integrated healthcare practices, aligning with global trends in integrative medicine.

The AyurcaseRxiv preprint archive is an independent component of AyurCeL which will publish the case narratives generated from the AyurCeL clinical case repository for quick dissemination to the research community. The contributing clinicians can further develop these case narratives for publication in research journals. Including a post-publication review tool and a preprint archive is a novel approach in Ayurveda. These features not only facilitate quick dissemination of knowledge but also ensure that the published case reports adhere to established guidelines, thereby enhancing the credibility and scientific rigour of the content.

AyurCeL makes it easy for clinicians to document and archive their clinical experiences. In the first phase of the project, AyurCeL has already compiled the targeted 100 clinical cases in the database. A workforce of researchers is needed to assist clinicians in further developing content that can be incorporated into the platform. The scalability of the AyurCeL platform is dependent on the strength of the manpower available, which would require long-term funding.

Nurturing clinical expertise and skills amongst Ayurvedic practitioners is a very important step towards the realisation of SDG 3. By making available curated clinical data on an easily accessible online platform, AyurCel can fulfil SDG Target 4.3 of ensuring equal access to affordable technical, vocational, and higher education. By providing case studies, research, and practical examples, AyurCeL can become a tool for students and practitioners who are pursuing formal education in traditional medicine. It can also fulfil the SDG Target 4.4 of increasing the number of people with relevant skills for financial success by focusing on development of diagnostic and treatment skills in Ayurveda. The platform can offer training modules, interactive case studies, and certification programs that help individuals gain practical skills in Ayurveda, thereby increasing their employability and entrepreneurship opportunities in the field of traditional medicine.

Future work
As the AyurCeL platform evolves, conducting empirical studies to assess its impact on clinical outcomes and educational efficacy would be beneficial. This could provide valuable insights for further refining and expanding the platform's features. There is also scope and possibility to incorporate AI-enabled features into this platform. To achieve SDG 3 “Good health and well-being,” the support of Traditional, Complementary, and Integrative Medicine (TCIM) is vital, as a significant portion of the global population relies on it to address health-related concerns. Patwardhan B. et al. highlight the significance of leveraging technology to enhance the understanding of traditional systems and integrate them with conventional medicine, thereby advancing healthcare outcomes [14].

There are noteworthy initiatives showcasing the utilization of technology to enhance healthcare accessibility in rural areas. One such endeavour is ‘Amritajeevanam,’ a personalized health monitoring system designed for managing well-being in rural communities [15]. Integration of data mining tools with the AyurCeL database can help in generating deeper insights about trends in Ayurvedic clinical practice. There are several examples of the use of technology in Ayurveda, with AyurCeL being a pioneering effort for clinical case documentation.

E-learning tools like the Medical Virtual Patient Simulator (MedVPS) play a crucial role in enhancing medical education by offering immersive experiences in skills such as examination, diagnosis, and treatment. These simulations, structured around virtual cases with targeted learning objectives, enable more efficient learning for medical students. Leveraging AyurCeL as a comprehensive repository of successfully treated Ayurvedic cases can enrich such virtual simulation models with real-world evidence. This personalized data, derived from actual clinical practice, holds greater value compared to conventional medical texts, offering students a more authentic and contextually relevant learning experience and is very much relevant to Ayurveda [16]. AyurCeL holds the potential to contribute reliable clinical data to an Ayurveda-based chatbot, facilitating responses to queries from users, including students, doctors, and the general public, regarding treatments. Efforts employing CNN approaches and machine learning technologies have already been initiated in this direction. This integration of AyurCeL with a chatbot would enable seamless access to evidence-based information on Ayurvedic treatments, enhancing healthcare awareness and support for users across diverse demographics [17].

The integration of technology, particularly leveraging machine learning (ML), artificial intelligence (AI), simulation, and virtual learning, has become increasingly urgent in various fields, including healthcare. Initiatives like DHARA, RUDRA, and TKDL have already begun exploring these avenues, aiming to enhance healthcare delivery and education. AyurCeL stands poised to become another essential technology-assisted platform in this landscape.

Conclusion
The AyurCeL portal represents an advancement in the field of Ayurveda, offering a structured and interactive platform for clinical knowledge sharing and continuous learning. The architecture is designed to be robust and scalable, focusing on user experience and security. Its emphasis on quality, interactivity, and multi-module approach makes it a versatile tool for educational and professional development in Ayurveda.

By facilitating the sharing of clinical experiences and well-documented case studies, it addresses the critical gap in nurturing clinical expertise among Ayurvedic practitioners. The platform provides assistance to clinicians to organise their clinical case experience into a structured narrative that can be published on a web-based platform. Its interactive features and query functions provide a dynamic environment for continuous professional development. This aligns with the lifelong learning paradigm, which is increasingly recognized as essential in healthcare professions. This is expected to have a ripple effect on the quality of healthcare delivery, thereby contributing to improved patient outcomes.

The stringent inclusion criteria and the post-publication review module add layers of quality assurance to the platform. This is a significant step towards enhancing the scientific rigour in Ayurvedic case reporting, elevating the field's credibility nationally and internationally.

The AyurCeL platform aligns with the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals, particularly SDG3 (Good Health and Well-being), by contributing to improving healthcare delivery through enhanced clinical expertise. Its focus on quality education and continuous learning also resonates with SDG4 (Quality Education).

The AyurCeL portal not only augments the platform's educational utility but also has broader implications for interdisciplinary collaboration and clinical research. Its comprehensive approach to addressing the gaps in clinical expertise, educational resources, and scientific rigour positions it as a game-changer for Ayurvedic healthcare in India and potentially beyond. As the platform matures, it holds the potential to set new standards in Ayurvedic practice, thereby contributing to the advancement of integrative medicine.

Ethical considerations
All cases are anonymised and with the patient's consent, and no sensitive patient data is saved in the system.

Declaration of generative AI in scientific writing
